title: Pawia pallida
intro: Pawia pallida, also known as the sea cucumber plant, is a marine angiosperm that belongs to the family Cymodoceaceae. The health benefits of Pawia pallida include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and provide antioxidant properties, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of Pawia pallida include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pawia pallida include extracts, capsules, and teas, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including gastrointestinal disorders and skin problems.
taxonomy: Pawia pallida is classified as a species of sea cucumber in the family Holoporidae. It belongs to the phylum Echinodermata and the class Holothuroidea. Pawia pallida is further classified as an order Aspidochiotea and a family Holoporidae. This classification places the species within the kingdom Animalia and the domain Eukarya. Pawia pallida is also known to be a part of the genus Pawia.
